item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is to be assumed that it is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), and the image has been removed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Perhaps a link to SCP-1121, which turns people into bugs?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert edits at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

So, a huge part of this article is the fact that it was Foundation-made. Site-45, in addition to this humanoid-bug man, also has SCP-2401 and SCP-439 in terms of insect based anomalies. It wouldn't be to far of a stretch for Dr. Wimble to have used DNA from these sources in addition to the ones listed in this skip in making 1598. In addition to an inline link to Site-45 in the containment procedures, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1598 is an organism measuring 4.5m in length, 3.9m in height when standing, and weighing approximately 5.2t. SCP-1598 displays physical and genetic characteristics of Lymantriids, Culicids, Tabanids, Gryllids, and Hominids1 in various stages of maturity. Apart from limbs, SCP-1598's surface is covered with irregular layers of chitinous plates, skin, urticating bristles, male/female genitalia, barbs, teeth, antennae/cerci, coarse hair, prehensile tongues, and occasionally underdeveloped wings. SCP-1598 appears to possess both an incomplete exoskeleton and endoskeleton, though both seem to function well when used in conjunction. SCP-1598 has no discernible head, but is seen to possess a multitude of eyes and oral/anal cavities of variable size across its body.

To this:

SCP-1598 is an organism measuring 4.5m in length, 3.9m in height when standing, and weighing approximately 5.2t. SCP-1598 displays physical and genetic characteristics of Lymantriids, Apinids, Culicids, Tabanids, Forficulidids, Gryllids, and Hominids2 in various stages of maturity. Apart from limbs, SCP-1598's surface is covered with irregular layers of chitinous plates, skin, urticating bristles, male/female genitalia, barbs, teeth, antennae/cerci, coarse hair, prehensile tongues, and occasionally underdeveloped wings. SCP-1598 appears to possess both an incomplete exoskeleton and endoskeleton, though both seem to function well when used in conjunction. SCP-1598 has no discernible head, but is seen to possess a multitude of eyes and oral/anal cavities of variable size across its body.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. tussock moths, mosquitoes, gadflies, crickets, and primates (specifically humans)
2. tussock moths, honeybees, mosquitoes, gadflies, earwigs, crickets, and primates (specifically humans)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the ffuuuuuuu

this has a good balance of information leaked and withheld to make a great mystery and reaction

Also confirms that old truism: Lab-6 is jerks. Total suck lab. I don't even know why we have a Lab-6.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This settles it: everyone in the Foundation universe needs a hug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do believe he's writing in his own crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i might just be missing it but i dont think its expressed in the article exactly how these messages are being left?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is both disturbing and sad. I'd never read it before. I thank the 'random sep' button, which is how I browse these days.

I looked up 'urticulating' to see if its a word. It doesn't appear so. If not, maybe fix it.
my mistake. 'urticating' is indeed a word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is unsettling as you pull back the layers. Came into this blind, so it only started to dawn on me as I read through it what had happened. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well done! Upvoted.
And that prior doctor was naughty, naughty, naughty. Too bad they wont listen to this guy about it.

Also:
"Prepare for unforseen consequences."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was wondering if someone was going to pick up on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the Kafka reference. ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man. Reading the description was fun, in a "what in the shitting fuck is this awful thing?" sort of thing.

Then I hit the level 2 addendum and I knew: we'd made this. It was human once.

Very well structured article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Small nitpick:

Foundation's head genetics department

Why the head genetic's department and not just any Foundation department of genetics?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously. There are a lot of times when I just skim the containment procedures, but here they really drew me in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh well done

YOUIDIOTS

I enjoyed reading this very much. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The foundation possibly doesn't like being called idiots and try to hide it so they don't have to bear the embarrassment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: right when "Whenever a new stomach appears" hit me like a ton of bricks, I knew this was going to be a wild ride.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I.. yup. Yup. Yup yup yup yup yup yup. +1. Just a few things.

inserted directly into the stomach through the "abdomen"

I don't believe the quotes are necessary. Abdominal area may work.

SCP-1598 consistently generates various appendages and internal organs quite rapidly

"quite rapidly" is a little off tone. Maybe 'at a rapid rate' may be better, albeit this is stylistic.

During altercations, SCP-1598 has shown an absolute refusal to submit to any attempt at conventional suppression to the point of threatening its own life.

I'm not sure what you mean here, unless you're saying it will run towards bullets or so? Maybe I'm just tired.

The following are selected samples of writings created by SCP-1598 in chronological order that are considered to be sensitive information.

You see, that all seems to flow into one message. While powerful, it also brings to question: did that thing shit out that much shit, or just write really tiny? How does one discern "YOUIDIOTS" as sensitive, as opposed to "THEBURDENISTOOMUCH"?

Still, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically the Doc was doing something he wasn't supposed to be doing and tried to hide it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I WANT TO KNOW MOOOOOOORRRRRRRRRRRE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, why wouldn't they test this on monkeys instead? Or was this another researcher?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yikes. I liked it in the forums and I like it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very neat … at least if I'm interpreting it right.
